reasoning: Grab Holdings remains uninitiated despite being Southeast Asia's dominant super-app with a compelling fintech pivot story. The stock is down 1.1% today and trades near multi-year lows around $3.64, creating an attractive entry point as the company approaches GAAP profitability. Recent Iran-US détente news benefiting financials sector-wide provides a macro tailwind, while Grab's underappreciated lending and digital banking expansion across 8 countries deserves deep analytical treatment.
title: Grab Holdings: Southeast Asia's Super-App at an Inflection Point — Fintech Monetization Begins
angle: Consensus still values Grab as a loss-making ride-hailing company competing with Gojek, but misses that financial services now contribute 20%+ of revenue with dramatically higher margins. The 700M+ population TAM across Indonesia, Vietnam, Philippines, Thailand, Malaysia, and Singapore represents the most underpenetrated fintech market globally. Liria sees Grab's lending book scaling 3x by 2028 as the real value driver, not mobility.
